decks.notes: This deck focuses on using a combination of creature cards to generate powerful synergies and combos. It utilizes cards like Carrion Feeder, Cauldron Familiar, and Goblin Bombardment to create a recurring loop of sacrificing and bringing back creatures. The deck also includes toolbox cards like Chord of Calling and Sylvan Safekeeper to search for specific creatures as needed. The key strategy is to constantly generate value from sacrificing and bringing back creatures, while also having access to a wide range of creatures to adapt to different situations.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly generate mana with cards like Ruby Medallion and rituals, then use Ral, Monsoon Mage's ability to copy spells and deal damage to the opponent. Past in Flames allows you to reuse spells from your graveyard, while Reckless Impulse and Valakut Awakening help you dig deeper into your deck. The deck also includes some control elements like Hex Magic and Wrenn's Resolve to disrupt your opponent's game plan. Ultimately, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces and finish off your opponent with a big Grapeshot or other spells.

This Modern Aggro-Blink deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures with powerful enter-the-battlefield effects, then using cards like Ephemerate and Solitude to repeatedly blink them for additional value. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal, while Relic of Progenitus helps to control graveyard strategies. Ketramose, the New Dawn and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd are the key creatures in this deck, providing both aggressive threats and card advantage. By combining aggressive creatures with blink effects, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with tempo and value.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with powerful abilities and using cards that allow them to reenter the battlefield or gain additional value when they do. It also includes a mix of removal spells and protection cards to control the board and protect key creatures. The deck aims to apply early pressure with aggressive creatures and then maintain tempo by blinking them in and out of play, overwhelming the opponent with value and versatility. The combination of aggro and blink elements creates a dynamic and adaptable playstyle that can catch opponents off guard.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with support from Goblin Bombardment and Lightning Bolt, this deck aims to deal damage early and often. With the addition of Seasoned Pyromancer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for card advantage and additional threats, this deck is designed to put pressure on the opponent from the start and maintain a fast-paced, aggressive strategy throughout the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing various cards and abilities to generate and protect powerful creatures, primarily focusing on Broodscale Bloodchief. It includes a mix of removal spells, creature pump effects, and ramp cards to support the combo. The deck also features Eldrazi creatures and lands to provide additional threats and mana acceleration. The goal is to assemble the pieces needed for the combo quickly while disrupting the opponent's game plan and overwhelming them with formidable creatures.

The deck focuses on manipulating the graveyard and cycling through cards to eventually cast Living End, which will bring back all creatures from the graveyard, typically overwhelming the opponent with powerful monsters. The deck includes various ways to control the board and disrupt the opponent's plans while building towards the combo finish. It also includes creatures and spells to provide additional advantage and pressure to ensure victory once the Living End combo is executed successfully. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by efficiently utilizing graveyard resources and maintaining tempo throughout the game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly cycle through the deck using cards like Street Wraith and Shardless Agent while controlling the board with cards like Subtlety and Endurance. The ultimate goal is to cast Living End to bring back all the creatures from the graveyard, overwhelming the opponent. Cards like Violent Outburst and Generous Ent help to cascade into Living End efficiently. The deck also includes cards like Force of Negation to counter opponent's threats and Curator of Mysteries to provide additional card advantage and control.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Shadow of Doubt while controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Force of Despair. The deck aims to quickly deplete the opponent's resources and finish the game with powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Boggart Trawler. The graveyard is also utilized as a resource with cards like Necrodominance and Graveyard Trespasser. The deck focuses on maintaining pressure and control throughout the game to secure victory.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful synergy between the Broodscale Bloodchief creature and various support cards to generate a combo win condition. It features a mix of ramp cards, removal spells, and creature buffs to establish board control and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of Broodscale's abilities and other creature effects. The goal is to assemble the key pieces of the combo quickly and efficiently while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of disruptive spells and powerful artifacts to quickly assemble a win condition without needing to rely on traditional lands. By countering opponent's spells, ramping up mana with Lotus Bloom, and searching for key pieces with Whir of Invention, the deck aims to ultimately cast Goblin Charbelcher and activate it to deal massive damage directly to the opponent. The deck also includes a variety of control elements like Counterspell and Force of Negation to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while controlling the board with counterspells and removal. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also having a mix of creatures like Murktide Regent and Psychic Frog for threats and card advantage. With a variety of hand disruption spells, counterspells, and removal options, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver its opponent by controlling the game and slowly grinding out a win.

This mono black aggro deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand and board while applying pressure with powerful creatures like Emperor of Bones and Thought-Knot Seer. It also utilizes Urza's Saga to find key artifacts like Shadowspear and Nihil Spellbomb to control the board and gain an advantage. The deck aims to be proactive and efficient in its plays, leveraging cards like Mishra's Bauble, Fatal Push, and Kozilek's Command to gain tempo and maintain dominance in the game.

The deck focuses on using artifacts efficiently to assemble a powerful board presence quickly and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. It leverages cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to ramp up and generate advantage, while also utilizing artifacts like Urza's Saga and Weapons Manufacturing to generate value and create multiple threats. The inclusion of cards like Engineered Explosives and Claws of Gix allows for flexibility in dealing with threats and adjusting the game plan as needed. The deck aims to establish a dominant position on the board early and maintain pressure throughout the game.

The deck focuses on using graveyard interaction and reanimation spells to bring powerful creatures onto the battlefield quickly, while also controlling the opponent's resources with counterspells and removal. Key cards include Faithless Looting for card selection, Archon of Cruelty for a devastating reanimation target, and Unearth for recurring creatures from the graveyard. The deck aims to win through explosive creature plays and disrupting the opponent's game plan, creating a proactive and versatile strategy in the fast-paced Modern format.

The main concept of this Ruby Storm deck is to generate as much mana as possible using cards like Pyretic Ritual, Desperate Ritual, and Ruby Medallion in order to cast high-cost spells quickly. Ral, Monsoon Mage and Wrenn's Resolve are used to draw cards and maintain hand advantage. Past in Flames allows for the reuse of spells from the graveyard, while Wish provides flexibility to find key cards from the sideboard. The deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful combo to overwhelm the opponent and win the game.
